notable_excerpts: engagement from agents has been reasonably on the uptick. We added about 57,000 agents last year and we opened 60 offices
health remains one of the largest spend item for Indian consumers and it will only grow further in percentage spend of Indian households in coming years
We already have made the most difficult part of building the network for the OPD services
BFS and the insurance companies are currently in the process of getting regulatory approvals from both CCI and IRDAI
Bajaj Finserv prepares its financials on Ind AS basis; however, the insurance companies are currently not covered under Ind AS
we now have 100%, a lot more leeway to look at strategic opportunities which may involve dilution

chain_of_thought: The earnings call transcript for Bajaj Finserv Q4 FY25 shows limited ESG content. There are no mentions of environmental themes. Social aspects are lightly touched upon, with mentions of community engagement through agent expansion and healthcare services. Governance is discussed in terms of regulatory compliance and strategic flexibility, but lacks depth in areas like board independence or ethics.
